sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 2.50 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.2)

statement.latex: (A.\,Selberg). Let$$be an irreducible discrete subgroup of a connected Lie group$G$such that the factor space$G/$is non-compact but has finite volume in the Haar measure. Prove that$$contains a non-trivial unipotent element. { V.\,P.\,Platonov} This is proved (D.\,A.\,Kazhdan, G.\,A.\,Margulis, Math. USSR--Sb., { 4} (1968), 147--152).

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory VI_BRIDGING; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
